clusters.headline: South Africa: Madlanga Commission to Rule on Request for Private Testimony - South African News Briefs - July 8, 2026
Egypt: Brave Egypt Fall Just Short As Argentina Stage Dramatic Comeback to Reach World Cup Quarter-Finals
clusters.summary: [CAF] Egypt's FIFA World Cup dream ended in heartbreaking fashion on Tuesday after the Pharaohs produced a heroic performance before eventually succumbing 3-2 to defending champions Argentina in a dramatic Round of 16 encounter in Atlanta.

clusters.headline: Ghana halts visit by South African president amid row over anti-migrant protests
clusters.summary: Many in Ghana feared his presence would lead to mass protests, and officials say it is postponed for now.

clusters.headline: Woman suspected of Monaco bomb attack found dead in Ukraine
clusters.summary: Police had been hunting Anastasiia Berezovska after a Ukrainian millionaire was injured in the blast.

clusters.headline: Middle East states should not allow their territories to be used to attack Iran, foreign ministry warns
clusters.summary:

clusters.headline: Man dies of heart disease in Woodlands flat after release from jail for killing temple worker: Coroner
clusters.summary: Loh Suan Lit had bludgeoned a temple assistant to death in 2016.

clusters.headline: ‘Abandoned and left to die’: Family of Hussam Abu Safiya says he has been betrayed by rights groups

clusters.headline: Kenya: Activists Push for Parliamentary Probe Into Alleged Rights Violations By Security Agencies
clusters.summary: [Capital FM] Nairobi -- Human rights campaigners are seeking the formation of a joint parliamentary committee to investigate allegations of extrajudicial killings, enforced disappearances, torture, abductions and the excessive use of force by security agencies.

clusters.headline: Singer Wu Chun returns to Singapore phone booth where he tearfully called his wife 30 years ago
clusters.summary: The former Fahrenheit member and actor made a sentimental detour to an old public phone booth outside Ngee Ann City along Orchard Road.

clusters.headline: Trains and emergency calls affected after major outage at Australia's largest telecoms company
clusters.summary: Servers at data centres in Sydney and Melbourne were to blame but the exact cause remains unknown.

clusters.headline: Top Democrats press Maine Senate candidate to drop out of race over sexual assault allegation
clusters.summary: Graham Platner denied the allegation, calling it "categorically false" but said he was "taking the time to reflect on the best path forward".

clusters.headline: Taiwan's premium chocolate makers earn global recognition but climate change threatens growth
clusters.summary: In the second of a three-part series on Taiwan beyond semiconductors, CNA explores how the island’s emerging premium chocolate industry is balancing global success with growing climate pressures.
